mission.title: cert-manager: Support ACME certificate profile selection (e.g. tlsserver, shortlived)
mission.description: Support ACME certificate profile selection (e.g. tlsserver, shortlived). Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Let's Encrypt is extending the ACME protocol to include "certificate profiles": https://letsencrypt.org/2025/01/09/acme-profiles/

By selecting a certificate profile, certificates with different traits can be generated by the CA.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support ACME certificate profile selection (e.g. tlsserver,…
steps.description:
📢 A new pre-release is available which contains this fix or feature: 
 * https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/tag/v1.18.0-beta.0. 
 
Please test and report back.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support ACME certificate profile selection (e.g. tlsserver,…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
📢 A new pre-release is available which contains this fix or feature: 
 * https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/tag/v1.18.0-beta.0. 
 
Please test and report back.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
